name: Wolverine Stack
subtitle: BPC-157 + TB-500 | Tissue Repair & Recovery Protocol
overview: Combines BPC-157 and TB-500 for synergistic tissue repair and recovery. 87.5% improvement in knee pain patients per Lee & Padgett 2021 study.
mechanism: BPC-157 increases actin production and modulates nitric oxide for vascular effects; TB-500 sequesters actin for cell migration; synergistic enhancement of fibroblast and immune cell movement to injury sites.
keyBenefits: Reduced inflammation and enhanced cell migration

items.name: Tendon & Ligament Healing
items.description: Both peptides show evidence for accelerated repair; BPC-157 improved Achilles tendon healing in animal models.

items.name: Muscle Injury Recovery
items.description: BPC-157 demonstrates enhanced muscle regeneration; TB-500 promotes cell migration essential for repair.

items.name: Joint Pain & Cartilage
items.description: Lee & Padgett study showed 87.5% of knee pain patients improved with BPC-157/TB4 combination.

items.name: Accelerated Wound Closure
items.description: TB-500 Phase 2 trials showed approximately one month faster healing in ulcer patients.

items.name: Inflammatory Cytokine Reduction
items.description: Both peptides modulate inflammatory pathways through different mechanisms.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection recommended; most studied and effective route.

steps: BPC-157: Add 2mL BAC water to 5mg vial = 250mcg per 0.1mL
TB-500: Add 2mL BAC water to 5mg vial = 250mcg per 0.1mL
Inject slowly along vial wall; swirl gently—never shake
Do NOT mix peptides in same syringe unless pre-made blend
Use separate injection sites if administering same day

deliveryMethods.overview: BPC-157 stable in gastric acid; TB-500 oral bioavailability less established.

interactions.notes: BPC-157 upregulates GH receptors; combined use may accelerate recovery.
GH secretagogues complement stack by increasing endogenous growth hormone.
MGF activates satellite cells; combined mechanisms may enhance muscle recovery.
Copper peptide promotes collagen; no negative interactions expected.
BPC-157 shows protective effects against NSAID-induced damage; long-term effects unknown.
Steroids may counteract healing; BPC-157 shown to reverse some steroid-induced damage in models.
Both peptides promote angiogenesis; monitor for bleeding issues.
Angiogenesis promotion could theoretically support tumor growth; contraindicated with cancer history.

timeline.effects: Possible reduction in acute inflammation and pain at injury sites
Noticeable improvement in recovery between workouts; reduced soreness
Significant improvement in chronic injuries; improved joint comfort
Optimal healing effects; structural improvements in damaged tissues
Benefits may persist as healed tissue maintains integrity

common: Not extensively documented in human studies
stopSigns: Any unusual lumps, growths, or rapid tissue changes
Severe injection site reactions or infections
Signs of allergic reaction (rash, swelling, difficulty breathing)
contraindications: ANY history of cancer or suspicious growths
monitoring: Banned by WADA and major sports organizations

good: White or off-white lyophilized powder cake indicating proper freeze-drying
Crystal clear solution after reconstitution with bacteriostatic water
Certificate of Analysis with HPLC purity testing (>98%) and mass spectrometry verification
warning: Minor clumping acceptable if dissolves completely with gentle swirling
bad: Collapsed, yellowed, or powder stuck to vial sides—may indicate degradation from heat
Persistent cloudiness or visible particles after reconstitution—indicates contamination or degradation

references.title: BPC-157 + TB4 Knee Pain Study (Lee & Padgett)
references.keyFindings: 16 patients, intra-articular injection: Overall 87.5% (14/16) experienced knee pain relief. BPC-157 alone: 91.6% improvement (11/12). BPC-157 + TB4 combination: 75% improvement (3/4).

references.title: BPC-157 Systematic Review (Vasireddi et al.)
references.keyFindings: 36 articles reviewed. Preclinical models showed improvements in muscle tears, tendon ruptures, ligament tears, and fracture healing. Mechanism involves enhanced GH receptor expression and angiogenesis.

references.title: Thymosin β4 Clinical Wound Healing (Treadwell et al.)
references.keyFindings: Two Phase 2 clinical trials showed thymosin β4 accelerated wound healing by approximately one month in responsive ulcer patients.

references.title: Thymosin β4 Regenerative Properties Review (Goldstein et al.)
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review establishing thymosin β4 as regenerative, promoting cell migration, reducing inflammation/apoptosis, and decreasing scar formation.

references.title: BPC-157 Achilles Tendon Healing
references.keyFindings: Rats, 10 μg/kg i.p. daily, 14 days: BPC-157 demonstrated superior healing of transected Achilles tendons with improved biomechanical properties.

references.title: Thymosin β4 Wound Healing Acceleration
references.keyFindings: Rats, full thickness wounds: Topical or intraperitoneal Tβ4 increased re-epithelialization by 42% at 4 days and up to 61% at 7 days.

quickStats.typicalDose: BPC-157: 250-500 mcg per dose + TB-500: 2-2.5 mg per dose
quickStats.frequency: BPC-157: 1-2x daily; TB-500: 2x per week (typically Monday/Thursday or Tuesday/Friday)
quickStats.cycleDuration: 4-8 weeks for injury recovery; shorter for maintenance
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized powder: room temperature acceptable; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated for both peptides
